perturbation.original_text: All such information (whether written or oral) furnished (whether before, on or after the date hereof) by either company, or its directors, officers, employees, representatives (including, without limitation, financial advisors, financing sources, attorneys, consultants and accountants) or agents (collectively, “ Representatives ”) to the other party and all analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents prepared by the Parties or their Representatives in connection with their review of, or their interest in, the Transaction which contain, reflect, are generated from or are based upon, in whole or in part, any such information is hereinafter referred to as the “ Information. ”
perturbation.changed_text: All such information (whether written or oral) furnished (whether before, on or after the date hereof) by either company, or its directors, officers, employees, representatives to the other party and all analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents prepared by the Parties or their Representatives in connection with their review of, or their interest in, the Transaction which contain, reflect, are generated from or are based upon, in whole or in part, any such information is hereinafter referred to as the “ Information. ”
perturbation.explanation: By removing the list of representatives, '(including, without limitation, financial advisors, financing sources, attorneys, consultants and accountants) or agents (collectively, “ Representatives ”)' we are omitting the detailed list of entities that compose a representative. The contract still uses the term 'Representatives' but the definition of who is considered a 'Representative' is not fully defined here, creating an in-text contradiction. Specifically, section 1 discusses the responsibility of the Parties to reveal the Information to their Representatives, by removing this list the term 'Representatives' becomes ambigous. This creates an omission, because in Section 1, Representatives are still responsible for the confidential nature of the Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties and their Representatives (i) will keep the Information strictly confidential and will not (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with paragraph 2 below), without the prior written consent of the other party, disclose any Information in any manner whatsoever, and (ii) will not use any Information other than in connection with the Transaction; provided, however , that the Parties may reveal the Information to their Representatives (a) who need to know the Information for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction, (b) who are informed by the Parties of the confidential nature of the Information and are provided with a copy of this Agreement, (c) who agree not to disclose any of the Information to any other party and (d) who agree to act in accordance with the terms of this letter agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto. The Parties will cause their Representatives to observe the terms of this letter agreement, and they will be responsible for any breach of this agreement by any of their Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The change removes the explicit definition of 'Representatives' as including financial advisors, financing sources, attorneys, consultants, and accountants. The contradicted text relies on the term 'Representatives' having a clear meaning, as it states the Parties are responsible for any breach of the agreement by their Representatives, and that representatives must follow specific guidelines. The change creates ambiguity about who is considered a 'Representative', directly contradicting the section that outlines their responsibilities and the parties' liability for their actions.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge that, unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and COMSYS with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the COMPANY nor COMSYS will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Transaction and hereby waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with any Transaction. In addition, neither Party will be under any obligation to negotiate a definitive agreement, by virtue of this or any written or oral expression, discussion or negotiation with respect to such Transaction by any or either of the Representatives of the Parties except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge that, unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and COMSYS with respect to any Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the COMPANY nor COMSYS will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Transaction and hereby waive, in advance, any claims in connection with any Transaction. In addition, neither Party will be under any obligation to negotiate a definitive agreement, by virtue of this or any written or oral expression, discussion or negotiation with respect to such Transaction by any or either of the Representatives of the Parties except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(including, without limitation, breach of contract)' from the list of waived claims, a potential in-text contradiction is created. The agreement still states that there's no legal obligation unless a definitive agreement is reached. However, with the omission of 'breach of contract', it becomes unclear if claims *other* than breach of contract (e.g., fraud, misrepresentation) are still waived. The contradiction arises because section 6 states that a non-breaching party is entitled to remedies at law or equity, but the new change leaves unclear if a party could make a claim based on law or equity. If this is the case, section 5 contradicts section 6
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby agree that in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement, the breaching party agrees that the non-breaching party may be entitled to remedies available at law or equity. In the event that the non-breaching party successfully enforces the obligations of the breaching party hereunder, the breaching party shall reimburse the non-breaching party for all reasonable costs and expenses incurred by the non-breaching party in this regard.
perturbation.justification: The original text waived 'breach of contract' claims, strengthening the 'no legal obligation' clause. By removing this waiver, the changed text introduces ambiguity about whether claims *other* than breach of contract are still waived. This contradicts the 'remedies available at law or equity' clause in the contradicted text, because it's now unclear if a party could make a claim based on law or equity outside of a breach of contract claim. The removal directly undermines the original intention of waiving claims, leading to a potential conflict and the possibility of legal action the original agreement sought to prevent.

perturbation.original_text: Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Information and receiving Party agrees that disclosing Party and its employees and agents shall have no liability to receiving Party resulting from any use of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Information and receiving Party agrees that disclosing Party shall have no liability to receiving Party resulting from any use of the Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and its employees and agents' a contradiction is created with Section 12, which states the agreement is binding upon the Parties and their respective Representatives, successors and permitted assigns. The omission in section 7 now suggests only the disclosing party *itself* is shielded from liability. If an employee or agent provides inaccurate information on behalf of the disclosing party, and the receiving party suffers damages from using that information, it's unclear if the employee/agent is still liable, despite section 12 assigning them responsibility in another part of the contract. This creates an in-text contradiction because employees and agents form part of what is being called 'Representatives'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be enforceable by the Parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns and will be binding upon the Parties and their respective Representatives, successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text shields the disclosing party, its employees, and agents from liability. The changed text removes 'and its employees and agents', narrowing the liability shield to only the disclosing party itself. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which states the agreement is binding upon the Parties and their respective Representatives, successors and permitted assigns. 'Representatives' would include employees and agents. The changed text now implies they might be liable, while the contradicted text implies they are bound by the agreement, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties and supersedes all prior collateral communications, if any, regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement shall govern all communications between the Parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties and supersedes all prior collateral communications, if any, regarding the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'This Agreement shall govern all communications between the Parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed.', we are creating an in-text contradiction with Section 11. Without this provision, it's unclear whether communications *after* the agreement are still subject to the confidentiality clauses, especially if those communications pertain to the subject matter. This omission contradicts the intent of the Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement, creating uncertainty about the agreement's scope in time. This omission contradicts with the first part of the section, where it says This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties. If there's an understanding but subsequent communications are not to be governed by the agreement, it is contradicting itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is the complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the Parties and supersedes any and all other agreements, if any, between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and contains all of the covenants and agreements between the Parties with respect thereto. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties and supersedes all prior collateral communications, if any, regarding the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement governs communications between the parties until either party gives notice that subsequent communications will not be so governed. The changed text omits this sentence. The contradicted text emphasizes the agreement as the 'complete and exclusive statement' and containing the 'entire understanding'. By removing the provision that governs communications after the effective date, the changed text introduces ambiguity as to whether later communications relating to the subject matter are covered, which contradicts the initial claim of completeness and exclusivity of understanding embodied in the agreement, and with the fact that the agreement contains the entire understanding, as mentioned in the explanation. This is a strong contradiction.
